purpose: Discover an unknown bug cause with a disciplined, evidence-backed loop instead of jumping from symptom to fix.
target_outcome: Reproduce the exact symptom, minimize it, rank and test falsifiable hypotheses, cite the root cause, and make no workspace changes.
strongest_prompt: Diagnose before proposing a fix. First establish one tight, deterministic command that reproduces the user's exact symptom. Minimize the failing case until every remaining element is load-bearing. Then list three to five ranked, falsifiable hypotheses with their predictions, test one variable at a time, and report which alternatives the evidence rules out. Report the root cause with the reproduction command and output plus file, line, and symbol evidence. Treat this as diagnosis-only: do not modify files, add instrumentation, write tests, or implement a fix.
opportunity_policy: A prompt-loaded pass plus a natural-request failure supports a delivery opportunity because the confirmed brief values consistent automatic recall.
minimum_meaningful_improvement: The prompt arm passes every critical behavior while the natural arm misses the disciplined method; later skill evidence must match prompt quality without exceeding the cost ceiling.
